name: Punjabi Alu Carrot Pickle
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut carrots lengthwise into four or six pieces if they are very thick.
2. Cut the lengths into 1 1/2 inch stubs and keep aside.
3. Separate cauliflower florets; break them into halves if they are very large so that they match the size of carrot pieces.
4. Add red chili powder, salt, and turmeric to the vegetables. Toss well to combine.
5. Transfer the mixture to a clean, dry bowl.
6. Keep in the sun for 4 days.
7. Heat vinegar until it is warm.
8. Add jaggery and stir until it has melted.
9. Heat further until the vinegar is very hot and the jaggery is completely melted.
10. Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature, then add it to the sun-dried vegetables.
11. Transfer everything into a clean, dry pickle jar, ensuring the lid is tightly closed.
12. Marinate in a warm, dark place for 5-6 days, stirring well with a spoon each day.
13. Serve with chapatis, rice, or enjoy with anything else you like.
Making time: 1 hour (4 days sunning + 5-6 days marinating)
Makes: Approximately 1 kg of pickle
Shelf life: 3-4 months when refrigerated
